hook: ChatGPT keeps explaining things to you like you've never heard of the internet.
caption:
ChatGPT keeps explaining things to you like you've never heard of the internet.

You ask about go-to-market strategy and it starts with 'First, let's define what a market is.'

You ask about your launch email and it gives you a 12-step guide on 'what email marketing is and why it matters.'

You're not broken. The AI just has no idea who it's talking to.

By default, every prompt you send gets treated like it came from a complete stranger with zero context. So the model hedges. It over-explains. It assumes nothing.

That's not a bug in the AI — it's a bug in the setup.

When you give it a calibrated profile (your background, how you process information, what you already know, what you hate being told), the whole dynamic shifts.

Same question. Completely different answer.

Instead of a beginner's guide you didn't ask for, you get a direct response that meets you where you actually are.

The AI didn't get smarter. It just finally knew who it was talking to.

That's what bootfile.ai builds — a profile that travels with you so you stop starting from zero every single time.

Link in bio to build yours.
